reviews.title: Demanding passport data and banning devs for coding? Unacceptable👎
reviews.content:
I've been using Claude for months, mostly for brainstorming and code assistance in my indie game projects. Out of the blue, Anthropic asked me to upload a scan of my passport and other personal ID for "enhanced verification." I complied reluctantly, thinking it was a one-time security check.

Instead, they permanently locked my account a day later. Support gave me a canned response about "violating terms of service" – but when I pressed for details, they vaguely mentioned "automated usage patterns." The only automation was me writing scripts and testing game logic with the API – perfectly legitimate development work. No bots, no scraping, no abuse.

Now I can't access my chat history, saved prompts, or even my paid subscription credits. They refused to reinstate me or refund my money. Asking for sensitive passport data is already a huge red flag – but punishing a solo developer for actually using the platform to build things is beyond ridiculous.

If you're a coder or game developer, s

reviews.title: Watch your bill
reviews.content: Anthropic double billed me for months, I had no idea until I just happened to stumble upon it when it tried to link to an account totally separate from mine. What had happened was I tried to sign up through stripe, they declined, so I signed up through Apple, then both accounts started billing me. Anthropic CSRs were rude, accusatory and refused my refund request. Now I am out $900. Let’s also not forget every new model is a regression and dumber than the last.

reviews.title: Thank you for the deluge of spam phone calls!
reviews.content:
I elected to use Anthropic Claude via Mac OS . As requested, I provided my telephone number.  Fortunately the iPhone 'Silenced unknown phone call' forwards the spam calls to voicemail, which I do not bother to listen to. The total number of unknown callers is
 up to 149.  The total number of unknown calls is 168.
There is no chance that I will ever sign up for Claude Pro ($20 per month) nor Claude, Max ($124.99/$249.99 per month) 💸

reviews.title: Resume fraud?
reviews.content: I asked this AI to help me build a résumé. I declined providing my personal information. It accuse me of fraud. I don’t want to give my personal information to an AI. How does me asking you to build me a resume template now all of a sudden I’m committing fraud. Am I asking you for the blueprint to rob a bank. What type of AI is this. I put periods because I don’t even want an answer to these questions. It’s very rude to accuse somebody of doing something that they’re not doing it’s very weird to jump to a hyperactive conclusion that I’m trying to commit fraud by asking an AI to build me a résumé template so that I can put in my personal information myself. I don’t know what type of weirdo built this app but I would highly recommend people stop using it. Also, I’ve had iPhone since 2013 and this is honestly the first review I’ve ever made.

reviews.title: Bait and switch
reviews.content:
I've been using claude really enjoying it. Claude has been a very helpful AI assistant in a lot of areas of my business and I decided to get a paid tier and to make sure I never ran out of ran out of chat.
Today, however, in the middle of work, I suddenly got a message that I was out of time and out of messages and that I wouldn't be able to use Claude for six more hours. 

That is completely unacceptable. Perhaps if I had gotten some emails, warning me that things were gonna change I would've been prepared. But in the middle of workflow, I realized, and found out that Claude was not going to be reliable. And it had nothing to do with its capabilities and everything to do with the developers desire to make more money off of me and my time of need. 

For that reason, I would recommend against it until they change their business model. I understand the need for limit limits. But you don't implement them right in the middle of somebody's workflow. If this is going to what they advertise i

reviews.title: Absolute disgrace from a company that calls itself the future of AI.
reviews.content:
I’ve used dozens of apps on my iPad — productivity tools, note-taking apps, coding environments, you name it — and every single one of them figured out how to collapse a sidebar in landscape mode. Every. Single. One. It’s not a complex feature. It’s not cutting-edge technology. It’s a toggle button. A panel that slides away. Something developers have been implementing since the iPhone 3G era.

But not Claude. No no no. Anthropic — the company that wants to build artificial general intelligence and save humanity from existential risk — cannot figure out how to hide a sidebar on an iPad.

You know what’s ironic? I’m trying to USE this app to LEARN. To grow. To build skills. And the app itself is the biggest obstacle in my way. I sit down, get into the zone, rotate my iPad to landscape for a better view — and boom. Sidebar locked. Full screen? Forget it. Focus mode? Doesn’t exist. Just a giant panel of projects and artifacts mocking me on the left side while I’m trying to think.

Every ot
